title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot examines four newspaper clippings from a *Sunday Comet* series titled "Tragic Women" that belonged to the deceased Mrs. McKinty. The articles profile four women: Eva Kahn, who sought to protect her daughter from a shameful past; Janice Corrigan, a victim of marital abuse; Lily Garber, who killed her cousin as a child; and Vera Black, whose life involved criminal associations before she moved to a Commonwealth colony. Poirot notes that Mrs. McKinty, who rarely collected such items, had recently clipped these specific articles and purchased ink, suggesting she intended to write a letter.

Poirot arranges a secret meeting with Pamela Hosford, the author of the "Tragic Women" series, to inquire if Mrs. McKinty had contacted her regarding the articles. Hosford eventually recalls receiving a letter from a woman in "Broadwee" (possibly Broadhurst) written in poor handwriting. The writer claimed to know the location of a photograph similar to one featured in the newspaper and inquired about receiving payment for the information. Hosford explains that the newspaper had sent a standard, polite refusal to the wrong address. Poirot concludes that Mrs. McKinty, known for being nosy, had recognized a person in an old photograph as one of the women in the newspaper profiles and had attempted to sell this discovery.
